LSE-MS6-L4-1400P-MSVPA-TSP-IMR-02 • Grade 6 • Music — Elements and Expressive Qualities Related to Structure

The Slow Ending

Click   🎧   to listen.
Listen to a conversation between a girl and a boy. Listen carefully. You will hear the conversation only once. After listening, you will answer a question.
Listen to the Conversation
QUESTION:
Why did the boy add a slow ending to the song?
Choose the best answer. You may change your answer before submitting.
SMART SUPPORT:
Go back to the conversation. Construct a complete academic justification. State your claim about why the boy made the ending slow, support that claim with reasoning drawn directly from what he says about his grandma's voice after leaving home, and articulate the conclusion that follows about what the slow ending carries.
CA ELD PI.B.5  |  CA VAPA Music 6.Re7.2a  |  MS Visual & Performing Arts — Music Grade 6
